message:
fix(session_restore): wrap delete_session in a single transaction (ft-dqsev)

delete_session previously issued three separate DELETE statements
against mux_pane_state, session_checkpoints, and mux_sessions, each
in its own implicit rusqlite transaction. A mid-sequence failure
(lock contention, disk full, cancel between statements) left the
database torn:

  * pane_states deleted but checkpoints still present → next
    load_checkpoint_by_id returned Some(checkpoint) with an empty
    pane_states vec, producing a restored session that looked clean
    but carried no terminal state — silent data corruption.
  * checkpoints deleted but session row still present → list_sessions
    showed the session but load_latest_checkpoint returned None.

Other session_restore paths already wrap multi-statement work in
conn.transaction() (see lines 626, 673). delete_session was the
anomaly.

Fix: acquire a single transaction, run all three DELETEs against it,
commit at the end. A failure on any statement rolls back the whole
sequence atomically. Return-value contract (true if session row was
actually removed) is preserved.

## Verification

Full cargo test blocked on ft-golgl (pre-existing compile errors in
diagnostic.rs / outcome.rs / workflows/handlers.rs under the feature
flags needed for session_restore's test harness). Change is
mechanical — wraps three conn.execute calls in a transaction,
pattern lifted verbatim from the existing transactional helpers at
session_restore.rs:626 + :673. The existing
`delete_session_cascades` happy-path test (line 3938) continues to
pass conceptually because the fix does not alter success semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
